_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
alos: 5 days (Once diagnosis is established the case can be booked in the relevant package, further stay/admission should be decided based on the level of complications of the disease)
clinical_indications: Peak incidence 2-4 months. Spells can last from minutes to hours. Usually occurs in morning after crying, feeding or defecation
Sudden onset of cyanosis or deepening of cyanosis
Alterations in consciousness, encompassing a spectrum from irritability to syncope
Decrease in intensity or even disappearance of systolic murmur
Known or evidence suggesting structural heart disease
Later stages may lead to gasping respiration limpness, convulsion, cerebrovascular accident or even death

preauth.label: Complete blood count, serum ferritin, blood glucose

claim.label: Detailed Indoor case papers (ICPs) with treatment details
Detailed Discharge summary with follow-up advise at the time of discharge

ppd_preauth.q: Did 2 D Echo / Doppler confirm congenital heart disease?

common_queries: 2D Echo / Doppler report not provided or does not confirm congenital heart disease.

clinical_key_pointers: Cyanotic spells affect infants and young children from six months to five years of age. The onset is usually between 6 and 18 months of age and is almost always before two years of age.
Peak incidence 2-4 months. Spells can last from minutes to hours. Usually occurs in morning after crying, feeding or defecation
Sudden onset of cyanosis or deepening of cyanosis
Alterations in consciousness, encompassing a spectrum from irritability to syncope
Decrease in intensity or even disappearance of systolic murmur
Known or evidence suggesting structural heart disease
Later stages may lead to gasping respiration limpness, convulsion, cerebrovascular accident or even death
